title: Polestar Announces Leadership Change: Michael Lohscheller Appointed CEO
text: Polestar announced a leadership transition, with Michael Lohscheller appointed as Chief Executive Officer, effective October 1, 2024, replacing Thomas Ingenlath. Lohscheller brings extensive experience from his previous role leading operational and strategic initiatives at Volvo Cars. The leadership change marks a strategic shift for Polestar as the company navigates profitability challenges and intensifying EV market competition. Thomas Ingenlath, who served as CEO since Polestar's inception, successfully positioned the brand for its next chapter. Following the transition, Ingenlath was appointed to advise Polestar and Volvo's parent company, Geely. With Ingenlath's reappointment as Chief Design Officer at Volvo Cars, the Swedish brand reinforced its commitment to visionary design and electrification. Lohscheller's appointment signals a focus on operational efficiency, cost management, and strategic restructuring as Polestar works toward cash flow breakeven. The leadership transition occurs during a critical period for the EV manufacturer as it manages rising losses, mounting debt, and tariff pressures while advancing new product launches.
